items.title: Access all areas with 1 credential: Why Australian workplaces need identity convergence now
items.summary: Rising cyber threats and hybrid work are pushing Australian employers to replace scattered badges, passwords and tokens with one credential.

items.title: ‘Digital duty of care’ push puts AI accountability in focus
items.summary: Following the release of a new Federal Government discussion paper, calls are growing for stronger safeguards to ensure artificial intelligence is used responsibly across workplaces and society. The paper proposes [...]

items.title: Tenable adds continuous validation to exposure platform
items.summary: Security teams could cut wasted remediation work as the update helps separate blocked exposures from those attackers can still exploit.

items.title: Barracuda launches AI email protection for Microsoft 365
items.summary: A single phishing email can now compromise identities, bypass multifactor authentication and hit endpoints within five minutes, Barracuda said.

items.title: Pax8 adds inforcer for Microsoft 365 security tools
items.summary: The tie-up gives managed service providers a way to standardise Microsoft 365 security and prepare SMB customers for Copilot adoption.

items.title: Guidewire to host InsurPitch Sydney for insurtechs
items.summary: Australian insurtechs will gain a free route to pitch insurers and investors, with the winner earning an expenses-paid trip to Las Vegas.

items.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
items.summary: And one of those basics is focusing on sectors where a ransomware disruption creates immediate pressure to pay up, like with healthcare.

items.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
items.summary: Attackers are actively targeting various sectors across nearly 200 countries and already have compiled a list of working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised devices.

items.summary: Australian boards risk missing critical technology and supply chain vulnerabilities due to limited visibility over third-party and sub-tier digital dependencies, according to a new white paper from law firm Clayton [...]

items.summary: A cyber attack on Mackay Sugar, described as Australia’s second-largest exporter of sugar, has been claimed by a ransomware group known as “The Gentlemen”, which the email said has more [...]

items.title: Australian organisations urged to shift to autonomous cyber defence as AI threats accelerate
items.summary: Australian organisations are being urged to adopt more autonomous and proactive cyber defence approaches as artificial intelligence (AI) increases both the pace and sophistication of cyber threats, according to commentary [...]

items.title: Some text messages to look different from 1 July
items.summary: Australians will notice some text messages look different from 1 July, when new rules take effect aimed at reducing SMS impersonation scams, according to the Australian Communications and Media Authority [...]

items.title: Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials
items.summary: In addition to executing entirely in memory, the malware's infection chain incorporates other anti-analysis techniques designed to evade detection.

items.title: Security Community Slams US Ban on Exporting Mythos, Fable
items.summary: An open letter signed by dozens of security experts asked the government to reverse export restrictions on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.

items.title: Rokarolla Android Trojan Levels Up to Full Device Control, Persistence
items.summary: The emerging malware, spread via fake TikTok and Chrome downloads, has evolved by combining banking fraud with extensive device surveillance and remote control.

items.title: 'Lorem Ipsum' Malware Pivots to ClickFix Delivery
items.summary: New analysis shows the campaign, which uses compromised WordPress sites, may be linked to the ransomware and data extortion group Vice Society.

items.title: Willis report finds cyber insurance covers most average breach and first-party losses
items.summary: More than 95% of average data breach losses and 90% of average first-party losses are adequately covered by insurance, according to a new report by Willis, a WTW business. The [...]

items.title: NEC Australia and Exabeam expand partnership focused on behaviour analytics for security operations
items.summary: NEC Australia and Exabeam have expanded their strategic partnership, aiming to help Australian organisations strengthen security operations and address insider risk as automation and AI adoption increases. In a statement, [...]

items.title: HTTP/2 Bomb Attacks Put Telcos, Healthcare Orgs at Risk
items.summary: The denial-of-service (DoS) exploit takes advantage of two features in HTTP/2 that were designed to save Internet bandwith, not power massive amplification attacks.

items.title: Copilot 'SearchLeak' Attack Allows 1-Click Data Theft
items.summary: The critical, three-stage attack is now patched, but it's part of a new group of AI prompt-injection issues that use hidden URLs and other variables.

items.title: The Beginning of the End of Social Engineering
items.summary: AI-native operating systems are shifting the responsibility to stay vigilant against social engineering cyberattacks from the user onto the system itself.

items.title: It Is Trivially Easy to Use Reddit to Manipulate AI Search, Research Suggests
items.summary: "We show that a tiny snippet—just 13 words—of retrieved text on a UGC website like Reddit, Wikipedia, Quora, or Facebook can change AI agents to output spam / scam content pretty consistently."

items.title: The OPSEC Rave Wave (with Imani Thompson)
items.summary: We get into how platforms have tried to make surveillance cute, why that damn Duolingo owl emotionally manipulates you, and why learning about privacy best practices when surrounded by community works.

items.title: US Cracks Down on Anthropic AI Models Amid Abuse Concerns
items.summary: Anthropic abruptly suspended all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 after receiving an export control directive that banned foreign nationals from using the technology.

items.summary: Stay cool: Mythos 5 is an upgrade over Mythos Preview while Fable 5 is Mythos "made safe for general use," Anthropic explains.

items.title: Phishing Attack Volume Down 20%, But Risk Still Rising
items.summary: Hackers are valuing quality over quantity, using AI to upgrade their phishing attacks rather than multiply them.

items.title: Max-Severity Ivanti Flaw Exploited 24 Hours After Disclosure
items.summary: Initial methods suggest attackers had likely mapped out Ivanti's asset landscape upfront and acted quickly once the exploit became public.

items.title: Chatbots Keep Telling Stories About Lighthouse Keeper 'Elias Thorne'. We Might Know Why
items.summary: LLMs including ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude are obsessed with telling stories about lighthouse keepers and clockmakers, and one character named 'Elias Thorne' has made his way from chatbots to Amazon books. Researchers are trying to discover why.

items.title: Privacy Commissioner finds Optus breached privacy in White Pages listing matter
items.summary: Australia’s Privacy Commissioner Carly Kind has found Optus interfered with the privacy of tens of thousands of customers whose details were incorrectly published in the White Pages despite requests for [...]

items.title: Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.summary: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the nation's state-sponsored cybercrime groups, which target financial firms and other businesses.

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.summary: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.summary: Security research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.summary: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.summary: Australians planning to attend the FIFA World Cup in North America next year are being warned to watch for ticketing and related scams that typically surge around major sporting events, [...]

items.title: Blame AI: Patch Tuesday Hits Record 206 CVEs
items.summary: Voluminous patch updates could soon be the norm, as artificial intelligence accelerates the speed and scale of vulnerability discovery.

items.summary: The attacks stemmed from a GitHub account that was also compromised in a previous Miasma attack on Microsoft last month.

items.summary: Two separate campaigns target CVE-2025-8088, fixed last July, to conduct data theft and cyberespionage against military and government targets in Ukraine.

items.summary: The financially motivated group is combining vishing, IT impersonation, and in-person office intrusions to steal data and extort victims.

items.summary: An extension of the Geneva Conventions could impose restrictions on cyberwarfare under ceasefire conditions and close a major loophole in international conflict.

items.summary: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.summary: Organizations are growing serious about which nation's rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.
